name: Entry and Evidence Collection
start_when: overnight verification notes exist or can be reconstructed
deliverable: entry packet summarizing what landed and what is unclear
acceptance: changed file set is captured
verification results are collected or reconstructed
open conflicts are enumerated
post-cutover run state is classified as clean, partial, or fragmented

name: Reconciliation
deliverable: single-source-of-truth reconciliation across router, registry, guide, and aliases
acceptance: router, registry, and guide agree on the same skill map
legacy command files are clearly non-authoritative
duplicate-authority cases are removed or explicitly flagged
remaining mismatches are documented

name: Authority Policy
deliverable: explicit authority model for router, registry, guide, and aliases
acceptance: one authoritative surface is named for routing
derived and mirrored artifacts are identified
state files reflect the new ownership model
transitional surfaces have clear status

name: Alias and Advisor Policy
deliverable: settled alias policy plus normalized advisor inheritance and overrides
acceptance: legacy alias purpose is defined
advisor parent defaults are consistent
child advisor overrides are normalized
retirement path for aliases is documented

name: Teaching Reprioritization
start_when: advisor and alias decisions are settled enough to map teachings
deliverable: new adoption order for remaining hub teachings against the folder-based architecture
acceptance: remaining teachings are ranked against the new architecture
conflicts and blockers are called out
state backlog reflects the new ordering

name: Documentation and Cleanup
deliverable: final post-cutover report plus cleaned-up narrative surfaces
acceptance: repo docs explain the authority model
post-cutover report summarizes outcome and open issues
state and guide reflect the settled architecture
cleanup only removes clearly stale transitional language

name: Final Verification
deliverable: verification addendum confirming the repo is coherent after day-2 work
acceptance: JSON and JSONL still parse
router paths resolve
aliases point to real skills
no major capability remains ambiguously authored
open issues are listed explicitly
